semantic_description: Function array_min scans a nonempty integer array a of logical contents l and length n, initializes ret from a[0], then uses a for loop from i = 1 to n - 1 to keep ret as the minimum value seen in the prefix l[0..i). It returns a value that occurs at some valid index of l and is less than or equal to every element of l. The function only reads the input array and preserves IntArray::full(a, n, l). The boundary case n = 1 skips the loop and returns a[0].
